scenario: Orion Marketplace runs a Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot service for a retailer that activates 15000 jobs per day for service tasks of type serviceA. A platform engineer wants the shortest snippet that opens a subscription for that job type and runs the worker glue code without the team writing any explicit polling loop or worker registration boilerplate.
question: Which Camunda Spring Boot Starter feature opens the subscription for the job worker automatically?

options.text: Subclassing AbstractJobHandler and registering the bean with the Spring context causes the starter to open a subscription against the broker for the configured job type at application start-up
Annotating the application class with @EnableZeebeWorker and listing the job type in application.yaml causes the starter to long-poll for that job type without further code from the worker bean
Annotating a bean method with @JobWorker(type = "serviceA") lets the Spring integration start the job worker and open the subscription for that job type automatically in the background
Registering a Spring @Scheduled method with the job type as a parameter causes the starter to translate the schedule into a long-poll subscription against the broker for that job type

a.text: Incorrect. There is no documented AbstractJobHandler base class that the starter scans for subscriptions; the documented mechanism is the @JobWorker annotation on a bean method.

b.text: Incorrect. The documented mechanism is the @JobWorker annotation on a bean method, not an @EnableZeebeWorker plus YAML job-type list.

c.text: Correct. The Camunda Spring Boot Starter starts the job worker and opens the subscription automatically when a bean method is annotated with @JobWorker(type = ...); the snippet on the page shows the type set to serviceA.

d.text: Incorrect. @Scheduled drives time-based execution and is not used to translate into Zeebe long-poll subscriptions; @JobWorker is the documented integration.

explanation: With the Camunda Spring Boot Starter a developer only annotates a bean method with @JobWorker(type = "serviceA"). The starter takes care of starting the worker, opening the subscription, and long-polling the broker for jobs of that type, while the method body holds the business logic.

docs.title: Connecting the workflow engine with your world
